---
title: "Education"
---

<div class="timeline">

<div class="timeline-item">
<h3>Rollins School of Public Health, Emory University</h3>
<div class="timeline-date">Aug 2024 — June 2026</div>
<div class="timeline-location">Atlanta, GA</div>
<p>Master of Public Health, Biostatistics<br>
Pursuing Data Science Certificate</p>
</div>

<div class="timeline-item">
<h3>College of Biological Science, UC Davis</h3>
<div class="timeline-date">July 2019 — June 2023</div>
<div class="timeline-location">Davis, CA</div>
<p>Bachelor of Science, Biological Science</p>
</div>

</div>

## My MPH in Numbers

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.height=4}
library(plotly)

# Grade distribution donut
grade_data <- data.frame(
  grade = c("A", "B", "S (Pass)", "In Progress\n(Missing Data)"),
  count = c(18, 1, 5, 6),
  color = c("#1ABC9C", "#3498DB", "#95A5A6", "#BDC3C7")
)

p1 <- plot_ly(grade_data,
  labels = ~grade, values = ~count,
  type = "pie", hole = 0.5,
  marker = list(colors = grade_data$color,
                line = list(color = "#fff", width = 1)),
  textinfo = "label+value",
  textfont = list(color = "#333", size = 13),
  hoverinfo = "label+value+percent"
) %>%
  layout(
    title = list(text = "30 Courses | CUM GPA: 3.911",
                 font = list(color = "#2C3E50", size = 16)),
    showlegend = FALSE,
    plot_bgcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0)",
    paper_bgcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0)",
    margin = list(t = 50, b = 10)
  ) %>%
  config(displayModeBar = FALSE)

p1
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.height=4.5}
library(plotly)

# Courses by semester and department
courses <- data.frame(
  course = c("BIOS 506", "BIOS 510", "BIOS 531", "BIOS 545", "BIOS 590R",
             "EPI 530", "PUBH 500",
             "BIOS 507", "BIOS 511", "BIOS 590R", "BSHES 500", "HPM 500",
             "PUBH 501", "DATA 530",
             "BIOS 521", "BIOS 525", "BIOS 580", "BIOS 585", "BIOS 590R",
             "DATA 550", "EH 500", "GH 500",
             "BIOS 520", "BIOS 581", "BIOS 590R", "BIOS 595",
             "DATA 534", "DATA 555"),
  semester = c(rep("Fall 2024", 7), rep("Spring 2025", 7),
               rep("Fall 2025", 8), rep("Spring 2026", 6)),
  dept = c("BIOS","BIOS","BIOS","BIOS","BIOS","EPI","PUBH",
           "BIOS","BIOS","BIOS","BSHES","HPM","PUBH","DATA",
           "BIOS","BIOS","BIOS","BIOS","BIOS","DATA","EH","GH",
           "BIOS","BIOS","BIOS","BIOS","DATA","DATA"),
  grade = c("A","A","A","A","S","B","S",
            "A","A","S","A","A","S","A",
            "A","A","A","A","S","A","A","A",
            "IP","IP","IP","IP","IP","IP"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

cats <- c("Biostatistics", "Data Science", "Epidemiology", "Public Health",
          "Behavioral Sciences", "Health Policy", "Environmental Health", "Global Health")
sems <- c("Fall 2024", "Spring 2025", "Fall 2025", "Spring 2026")

courses$category <- ifelse(courses$dept == "BIOS", "Biostatistics",
                    ifelse(courses$dept == "DATA", "Data Science",
                    ifelse(courses$dept == "EPI", "Epidemiology",
                    ifelse(courses$dept == "PUBH", "Public Health",
                    ifelse(courses$dept == "BSHES", "Behavioral Sciences",
                    ifelse(courses$dept == "HPM", "Health Policy",
                    ifelse(courses$dept == "EH", "Environmental Health",
                    ifelse(courses$dept == "GH", "Global Health", courses$dept))))))))

library(dplyr)
agg <- courses %>%
  group_by(semester, category) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop")

agg$semester <- factor(agg$semester, levels = sems)

dept_colors <- c("Biostatistics" = "#1ABC9C", "Data Science" = "#3498DB",
                 "Epidemiology" = "#E74C3C", "Public Health" = "#9B59B6",
                 "Behavioral Sciences" = "#F39C12", "Health Policy" = "#E67E22",
                 "Environmental Health" = "#2ECC71", "Global Health" = "#1F77B4")

p2 <- plot_ly()
for (cat in names(dept_colors)) {
  d <- agg %>% filter(category == cat)
  if (nrow(d) > 0) {
    p2 <- p2 %>% add_bars(data = d, x = ~semester, y = ~n, name = cat,
                           marker = list(color = dept_colors[[cat]]))
  }
}
p2 <- p2 %>%
  layout(
    barmode = "stack",
    title = list(text = "Courses by Semester & Department",
                 font = list(color = "#2C3E50", size = 16)),
    xaxis = list(title = "", tickfont = list(color = "#333")),
    yaxis = list(title = "Courses", tickfont = list(color = "#333"),
                 titlefont = list(color = "#333"), gridcolor = "#eee"),
    legend = list(font = list(color = "#333")),
    plot_bgcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0)",
    paper_bgcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0)",
    margin = list(t = 50, b = 10)
  ) %>%
  config(displayModeBar = FALSE)

p2
```
